UPDATE: Expanded image ratios are now available. Pros: ​Smooth performance for an early build. Cons / Areas for Improvement: ​No Upscaling on Download: Currently no option to upscale images/media upon export, impacting final quality. ​Trashed Items: The mobile app still shows images and videos placed in the trash via the web version. This clutters the UI and causes inconsistency between platforms.

The safety filters are extremely restrictive. I am trying to create some Cyberpunk style anime characters for a SFW comic I'm writing for myself in order to exercise my English writing. Their outfits aren't very revealing but yet, if a little skin is visible on the legs for example, or the outfit is tight your useless safety filters automatically think the character is underage, a prominent person, A REAL PERSON. HOW AN ANIME CHARACTER CAN BE A REAL PERSON?? All my characters are over 23.

Hello Google Flow Team, I would like to report an issue with the image-to-video feature in the beta app. When I upload a picture and generate a video, the final result often changes the original character too much. The face, body, clothes, style, or overall appearance sometimes become completely different from the uploaded image. The main problem is character consistency and image preservation. The generated video should stay closer to the original uploaded photo.

The latest update completely ruined Google Flow AI. Before, it was simple, smooth, and perfect for creators. The “What happen next” feature made long videos seamless and cinematic. Image-to-video was easy and beginner-friendly. Now the UI is confusing, messy, and frustrating. Even downloading often fails. Please properly review this update and bring back the old interface and workflow. The previous version was far better and much more creator-friendly.
The UI of this app is messy, hard to use and needs major ergonomic overhaul.
By far google is leaving all ai models behind with its Gemini. This one strengthens google's ai tool ecosystem further. Keep growing google.

I was waiting for this app, but so far, the rendering is poor. It doesn’t understand prompts, and generates videos completely different from the image. You finally included an 'Animate' feature. But you removed the Extend feature, a very key feature which helps to seemlessly blend clips together. Kindly replace it. Also, please add and activate the other features like Remove and Insert, which were not working in the Flow page.

Google Flow currently only supports a 1K photo generator, which feels far less practical than working on a desktop. When trying to generate front, back, side, or top-down views from an original image, the system often produces completely unrelated results. I attempted multiple times, but the outputs consistently failed. Since the 5/19/2026 update, the performance has become even worse to the point that the feature is essentially unusable.

Google Flow Beta is an amazing AI creative app with a clean and user-friendly interface. The AI generates impressive visuals and cinematic content quickly and accurately from simple prompts. It is fast, smooth, and very useful for creators, designers, and content makers. Although there are a few minor bugs because it is still in beta, the overall experience is excellent. Google has huge potential with this app, and future updates could make it one of the best AI creative tools available.

Promising tool, but needs refinement "Google Flow has a lot of potential for streamlining workflows, and I appreciate the clean interface. The integration capabilities are quite smooth, which makes it easy to set up basic tasks. However, I’ve noticed some latency issues during complex operations, and it can be a bit overwhelming for new users to navigate the advanced settings. It’s a good start, but I’m looking forward to more stability and perhaps some more intuitive tutorials in the upcoming!
